messages.content: Consult me how to proceed with shira’s contract and demands

Here’s a concise, step-by-step playbook for wrapping up Shira’s negotiation and getting unstuck:

1. Get her formal counter-offer on paper—fast  
   • Ask Shira to send you her “real ask” (base + bonus structure) by end of day (or latest tomorrow morning).  
   • Clarify: Is ₪12 K at 75 % FTE, or ₪12 K at her current 50 %? You need that straight.  

2. Lock in clear hours vs. compensation  
   • If she wants a true raise (not just more hours), decide whether to:  
     – Increase her base at 50 % FTE to ₪12 K; or  
     – Convert her to 75 % FTE at that rate.  
   • Spell it out in the draft contract: “₪X K base at Y % time, plus bonus up to ₪Z on KPIs.”  

3. Define and document the KPI-linked bonus plan  
   • Keep it simple and measurable—e.g.:  
     – Revenue target (e.g. clinic income ≥ ₪1 M/mo → 100 % bonus)  
     – Wait-time metric (avg. < 2 wks → full bonus; 2–3 wks → 50 %)  
     – Budget retention (maintain 60∶40 mix)  
   • State frequency and payout schedule (monthly/quarterly).  

4. Propose a 3-month trial window  
   • “Let’s treat the next 3 months as a pilot: you onboard these new responsibilities, we track performance against the KPIs, then reconvene.”  
   • This gives her runway to prove herself—and you guard against over-committing.  

5. Line up your Plan B now  
   • Begin spec’ing a full-time administrative/operations manager (internal or external) who can take off some of Shira’s plate.  
   • Explore splitting clinical-ops and admin-ops duties between two roles, if her scope proves too broad.  

6. Communicate to the team  
   • Tell Rena and Alex that you’ve asked Shira for a written proposal and set a tight deadline; share the high-level terms you’re discussing so everyone’s aligned.  
   • Make clear that you’ll only green-light the final contract once the numbers and FTE match budget.  

Sample next-day email to Shira:  
“Shira, thanks for last night’s conversation. To move forward, please send me by tomorrow 10 am:  
1. Your preferred base salary and FTE %  
2. Your proposed bonus structure (measurable KPIs & payout)  
Once I have that in writing, we’ll finalize your contract—on a 3-month pilot basis—and lock in our commitments. If you’d like to chat before sending, let me know. Looking forward to wrapping this up cleanly.”
